meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Press freedom worldwide falls to its lowest level in 25 years
items.summary: Reporters Without Borders warns that 'journalism is increasingly criminalised worldwide'.

items.title: Pakistan opens up road trade routes into Iran amid Hormuz blockade
items.summary: With shipping disrupted by US-Iran tensions, Islamabad activates overland corridor to move stranded cargo into Iran.

items.title: Myanmar’s blanket prison term reduction trims Aung San Suu Kyi’s sentence
items.summary: The deposed elected leader has been granted two amnesties this month, but her remaining sentence remains unclear.

items.title: Zaragoza goalkeeper given 13-game ban after derby punch provoked brawl
items.summary: Esteban Andrada banned for 13 matches and teammate Dani Tasende for two after pitch melee in La Liga 2.

items.title: Rockets defeat Lakers in Reaves’s return, force Game 6 in NBA playoffs
items.summary: Despite the return of star Austin Reaves, the LA Lakers were defeated by Houston Rockets in Game 5 of their series.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 62 as Trump asks Iran to ‘give up’?
items.summary: Trump says the US blockade of Iranian ports is working, urging Tehran to 'give up' amid tensions.

items.title: Could the US-Iran war become a protracted ‘frozen’ conflict?
items.summary: In the absence of a permanent deal, the war between the US and Iran is becoming one of attrition despite huge costs.

items.title: Iranian officials to miss FIFA Congress over alleged mistreatment in Canada
items.summary: Iran's Mehdi Taj-led delegation turns back at Toronto airport due to 'unacceptable behaviour of immigration officials'.

items.title: Why is India turning to crocodiles and snakes to ‘fence’ Bangladesh border?
items.summary: India wants to  the border with Bangladesh badly — even if it takes venomous snakes or crocodiles.

items.title: Europe’s little-known mutual defence clause: Is it a NATO substitute?
items.summary: Several European leaders have called for a discussion on using the bloc's mutual assistance clause amid US tensions.

items.title: New Zealand court denies bid by mosque mass shooter to appeal conviction
items.summary: Brenton Tarrant shot dead 51 Muslim worshippers, including children, at two Christchurch mosques in 2019.

items.title: Marathon record breaker Sabastian Sawe returns to hero’s welcome in Kenya
items.summary: Sabastian Sawe returned home to a hero’s welcome in Kenya after being the first to run a marathon in under two hours.

items.title: London police release bodycam of arrest after two Jewish men stabbed
items.summary: Police in the UK have released bodycam footage showing the arrest of a suspect after two Jewish men were stabbed.

items.title: Video emerges of heavy firefight in Mali
items.summary: Video has been published by Russian mercenaries showing a heavy gun battle between Mali’s military and rebels.

items.title: Oil prices soar on fears of long supply disruption, US siege of Iran ports
items.summary: US President Trump meets with oil firms over ways to minimise impact on fuel supplies amid US blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: US indicts Sinaloa governor, 9 others over Mexican drug cartel links
items.summary: Indictment alleges that Governor Ruben Rocha Moya and other current and former officials worked with cartel leaders.

items.title: Israel seizes Global Sumud Flotilla boats 1,000km away from Gaza
items.summary: Israeli forces have intercepted around a dozen Gaza-bound aid boats from the Global Sumud Flotilla.

items.title: Pro-government rally in Tehran demands end to US threats
items.summary: Hundreds of Iranians have rallied in Tehran to demand an end to US threats and the ongoing blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: Hundreds of Ultra-Orthodox Jews protest Israel’s military draft
items.summary: Hundreds of Ultra-Orthodox Jews blocked a main road, as part of protests against Israel’s mandatory military service.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump urges Tehran to ‘give up’; Ghalibaf mocks US strategy
items.summary: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf ridicules US's economic pressure campaign as oil prices surge and Hormuz standoff continues.

items.title: Chile police blast water cannons at student protesters
items.summary: Chile’s police has cracked down on student protests against the govt's proposals to limit free higher education.

items.title: Carney ‘strong’ in year one, now must deliver on promises in Canada
items.summary: Canada's prime minister has used Trump's attacks on allies and others to refocus Ottawa's foreign policy.

items.title: Israel has begun intercepting Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla aid boats
items.summary: Organisers of a Gaza-bound aid mission say their vessels are being intercepted by Israeli military speedboats.

items.title: Israeli forces raid Global Sumud Flotilla boats in international waters
items.summary: Twenty-two out of 58 vessels were captured by Israel near Crete, as flotilla aims to deliver aid to Gaza under blockade.

items.title: UAE quits OPEC
items.summary: The UAE announced it’s withdrawing from OPEC on May 1st.
